title: 'reference does not exist' when accessing local images
body:
Hello,

I am trying to use skopeo to move images from one registry to another. Namely from my local repository to a registry server, but I always get an error 'Error response from daemon: reference does not exist'. The error persists with all other skopeo operations.

Steps to reproduce:

### Dockerfile
```
# Create Image with 3 Layers
FROM alpine:latest
RUN touch "1"
RUN touch "2"
RUN touch "3"
```

### Command history
`docker build -t testing:first  .`
```
...
 => => writing image sha256:18e0c93457665f92c1151665cee65e8ea3f35e58af2f9781b0b2412d1459dc87                            0.0s
 => => naming to docker.io/library/testing:first                                                                        0.0s

```

`docker images testing`
```
REPOSITORY   TAG       IMAGE ID       CREATED          SIZE
testing      first     18e0c9345766   18 minutes ago   7.32MB
```

`skopeo inspect docker-daemon:testing:first`
```
FATA[0000] Error parsing image name "docker-daemon:testing:first": loading image from docker engine: Error response from daemon: reference does not exist
```

`skopeo inspect docker-daemon:sha256:18e0c93457665f92c1151665cee65e8ea3f35e58af2f9781b0b2412d1459dc87`
```
FATA[0000] Error parsing image name "docker-daemon:sha256:18e0c93457665f92c1151665cee65e8ea3f35e58af2f9781b0b2412d1459dc87": loading image from docker engine: Error response from daemon: failed to get digest sha256:18e0c93457665f92c1151665cee65e8ea3f35e58af2f9781b0b2412d1459dc87: open /var/lib/docker/image/overlay2/imagedb/content/sha256/18e0c93457665f92c1151665cee65e8ea3f35e58af2f9781b0b2412d1459dc87: no such file or directory 
```

### System information
`uname -a`
```
Linux ... 5.19.0-46-generic #47~22.04.1-Ubuntu SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Wed Jun 21 15:35:31 UTC 2 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

`docker --version`
```
Docker version 24.0.2, build cb74dfc
```

`skopeo --version`
```
skopeo version 1.4.1
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I’m sorry about the late response.

Testing this myself, with
```console
# rpm -q moby-engine skopeo
moby-engine-20.10.23-1.fc38.x86_64
skopeo-1.13.1-1.fc38.x86_64
# skopeo inspect docker-daemon:testing:first
{
    "Name": "docker.io/library/testing",
…
# docker inspect testing:first # to get the "Id" value
[
    {
        "Id": "sha256:6461d3e2b046578286456d2a072d9be996ee31d394e2a5141551aeb22652d8d0",
…
# skopeo inspect docker-daemon:sha256:6461d3e2b046578286456d2a072d9be996ee31d394e2a5141551aeb22652d8d0
{
    "Digest": "sha256:6c92b167c09e82f98116c5747ad6f387a4bbaf5fc25fb38ab2321863364a343a",
…
# skopeo copy docker-daemon:sha256:6461d3e2b046578286456d2a072d9be996ee31d394e2a5141551aeb22652d8d0  dir:t
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d630ec0a7bb5 done  
Copying blob 675b685ddcd0 done  
Copying blob 78a822fe2a2d done  
Copying blob 4857730ca852 done  
Copying config 6461d3e2b0 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
```

This all seems to work as expected.

Same when `docker-ce-24.0.5-1.fc38.x86_64`… but I didn’t test that old Skopeo version.

My best guess is that the two commands are referring to a different Docker server; Docker itself, IIRC, respects the `DOCKER_HOST` environment variable, but Skopeo requires a `--daemon-host` option.

Hello @mtrmac ,

thank you, that is a good point. Apparently I forgot to disable one of my daemons. By using the `--daemon-host` argument I get the expected behaviour.
